entry.title: Requirement – Credit Management for Cement Sales Orders
entry.content:
We have a requirement related to Credit Management in SAP S/4HANA RISE.

Requirement:
If a customer’s overdue is ≥ 90 days, the system should not allow creation of a Sales Order. This restriction should be applicable only for Cement Sales. For other materials, no change is required and the process should remain as is.

Current Business Scenario:

  • We are currently using credit management at the delivery level (Credit Group 02 – Delivery).
  • There is no restriction during Sales Order creation; all checks are applied at the time of delivery creation.
  • For oldest open items, a limit of 180 days is maintained, beyond which the system restricts processing.

New Requirement:
If the customer’s oldest open item is greater than 90 days, the system should not allow Sales Order creation specifically for Cement materials. For other materials (such as TMT, Wire Rod, etc.), the process should continue as per the current business.

Kindly suggest how this requirement can be fulfilled.
Also, please advise whether creating a separate order type for Cement Sales is required, as currently we are using the same order type for all domestic sales.

@Lakshmipathi Sir, please suggest & let me know if any additional info required from my end.

SD (Sales and Distribution) SD Billing 

Regards,

Soumya Nath.

entry.title: Re: Requirement – Credit Management for Cement Sales Orders
entry.content: You can fulfill this requirement in SAP S/4HANA RISE by enhancing credit management rules to trigger a credit check at Sales Order creation specifically for Cement materials. This can be achieved either by using material-specific credit check configurations (via risk category or product hierarchy) or by introducing a separate order type for Cement sales. A separate order type is not strictly mandatory, but it often simplifies configuration and reporting.

entry.title: Re: Requirement is to create multiple inspection lots for...
entry.content:
SAP standard does not create multiple inspection lots for the same PO item + batch combination. This is consistent across on-premise and public cloud.

The behavior you are seeing is actually standard QM integration. When you post multiple partial goods receipts against the same PO line and batch, the system updates the same inspection lot rather than creating new ones. This is by design and the inspection lot is created at the combination of PO item + batch + inspection type, so subsequent GRs for the same batch are merged.

Below are the possible approaches

1) Batch Differentiation
If you want separate inspection lots, you must post the subsequent GRs with a different batch number. Each batch triggers a new inspection lot.

2) Inspection Lot Creation Settings
Check QM customizing for inspection lot creation (Quality Management → Quality Inspection → Inspection Lot Creation). But in S/4HANA Public Cloud, customizing options are limited compared to on-premise.

3) BAdI Enhancement
In public cloud, you can explore available Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for inspection lot creation. For example, BADI_QM_LOT_CREATE (on-premise) allows influencing lot creation logic. In S/4HC, check if equivalent released BAdIs exist in the Extensibility Catalog. You could add logic that if partial GR for same batch, force creation of new inspection lot.

4) Process Adjustment
If business requires separate lots for each partial GR, consider changing the process to always use unique batches per receipt. This aligns with SAP’s intended design.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content: @Venkat_Vyza thanks for your question. All other standalone offerings of SAP Build are still available as well as within the SAP Build offering as announced last year April. SAP Build Apps will be deprecated and is in maintenance mode. Please see the FAQs in the blog post which tools to use under which circumstances. The SAP Build Apps part within the SAP Build offering is only a fraction of the standalone offering - see here. Therefore, we highly recommend using the pro-code offering as of now for urgent projects. And also in the SAP Build offering there will be no additional SAP Build Apps features.

entry.title: Re: Requirement – Credit Management for Cement Sales Orders
entry.content: Sir, thank you for your response. Could you please guide me on how to trigger a credit check at the time of Sales Order creation specifically for Cement materials? Challenges Faced: 1. Single Customer for Multiple Products o Same customer is used for Cement as well as other products. o In SAP Transaction BP, the Check Rule is maintained at customer level, so it applies globally. o Hence, we are unable to differentiate credit behavior based on material using standard configuration. 2. No Check Rule Control in OVA8 (FSCM) o In S/4HANA FSCM, OVA8 does not contain Check Rule assignment, only:  Credit Control Area  Risk Category  Credit Group o Therefore, we cannot assign different check rules (e.g., 90 days vs 180 days) based on process or material via OVA8. 3. Material-Based Control Not Available in Standard o Standard FSCM logic is customer-centric, not material-specific. o We need product-specific credit control, which is not directly achievable via standard config. 4. Order Type Limitation o We are currently using a single order type for all domestic sales. o Even if we create a separate order type for Cement:  Users can still create orders with other order types and include Cement materials  This creates a control gap

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud PR Workflow
entry.content:
1. Workflow Substitution
Use the My Inbox substitution feature. Another user can be nominated as a substitute approver for the requester. When the workflow step reaches the requester (who is excluded), the substitute will receive the task.

2. Workflow Configuration – Dynamic Agent Determination
In the workflow configuration, you can add logic to check if approver = requester → reassign to alternate approver (e.g., manager or predefined role). This can be achieved using responsibility rules or BAdI for agent determination (if released in Public Cloud). In S/4HANA Cloud, check the Business Workflow Extensibility Catalog for available BAdIs such as DetermineApprover for PR workflows.

3. Escalation / Error Handling
Configure escalation paths in the workflow. If the system encounters an error (approver = requester), it can escalate to the next level approver automatically. This avoids manual intervention.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud PR Workflow
entry.content:
Dear @Ashley_Hung,

For Public Cloud if you configure Exclude Requestors and the PR requestor is also the only approver for that step, SAP states that if no recipients are determined after exclusion the workflow goes into Error unless the step is marked optional

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/bacc21e14d6c4b0fbf53dca3218abdd5.html

In that situation the standard recovery is not to directly reassign the PR document itself. The standard admin action is to go to Flexible Workflow Administration and use Resume after Error / Resume Workflow after you have corrected the recipient determination so that a valid approver can now be found. SAP documents both the app capability and that resuming re-triggers the workflow from the failed point

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/4b818514f0a2453bb72febf44b217f71.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/5d505ae2259e409b8487a4dbaa3b6cc4.html

Summary:

SAP explicitly says you can forward tasks of one user to another user for tasks that are ready for processing so if your PR is already in Error because no approver could be determined, forwarding is usually not the fix because no valid approval task was created for a real approver in the first place

Also SAP supports:

  • marking the step as optional, so it is skipped if no approver is found and
  • using custom agent determination for PR workflow via Badi MMPUR_WORKFLOW_AGENTS_V2, which is the standard extensibility option to derive an alternative approver

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/2966c6fc0b2c4667bd1511f974f187fe.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/b2cd80f3d0e94efd806c3f535b5395fa.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/31cc91282192493cbb651c132d966811.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/720dcec59d264114bc4ec19759aa4514.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/5ad74a497e8b4c8c8f1b10f705fe4e00.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: eSocial - Nota Técnica Nº 06/2026
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,  

Comunicamos a entrega das atualizações relacionadas à Nota Técnica Nº 06/2026 do eSocial S-1.3, que traz importantes modificações para garantir a conformidade legal. 

Principais Modificações 

Validação da Data de Comunicação de Óbito nos Eventos de Emprego 

O sistema agora valida algumas datas chave dos eventos de empregado contra a data de comunicação de óbito do empregado. 

Eventos afetados: 

  • S-2190 - Admissão de trabalhador - Registro preliminar 
  • S-2200 - Cadastramento Inicial do Vínculo e Admissão/Ingresso de Trabalhador 
  • S-2299 - Desligamento 
  • S-2300 - Trabalhador sem vínculo de emprego - início 
  • S-2399 - Trabalhador sem vínculo de emprego - término 

Quando qualquer data-chave desses eventos (como datas de admissão, transferência ou desligamento) for posterior à data de comunicação de óbito, o sistema gera mensagens de aviso para correção dos dados antes da submissão. 

Novo Campo infoPatPrec no Evento S-2500 

O evento S-2500 - Processo Trabalhista foi atualizado com o novo campo infoPatPrec, conforme especificado na NT 06/2026. 

SAP Notes Entregues 

As modificações estão disponíveis nas seguintes SAP Notes: 

  • 3726139 - eSocial - NT 06/2026 - Employee death date validations 
  • 3723879 - eSocial - NT 06/2026 - S-2500 - Transformation object for labor lawsuit 
  • 3724389 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3723879 

Lembrando que todas as entregas referentes ao eSocial você encontrará na SAP Note 3700197 - eSocial - Overview - 2026. 

Gostou desse post?  

Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas.  

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias. 

Um abraço,  
Ramiro Manoel 
Time de HCM Brasil

entry.title: "3-Dot" Loading Hang in Manage Replication App within SFM: Solving HTTP Error 431
entry.content:
Hi SAP Sustainability Community, I hope you're all doing great.

Have you ever encountered a situation where the Manage Replications app in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM) stays stuck on the loading screen with three animated dots?

While this might look like a system outage, our technical investigation into recent cases has identified a common root cause that is actually browser-related: HTTP Error 431 - Request Header Fields Too Large.

The Technical Breakdown:
This is not a functional bug within the SFM application logic. Instead, it occurs when the browser's request headers—usually due to an accumulation of cookies, session tokens, or long-lived authentication data—become too "heavy" for the server to process. The server then rejects the metadata handshake, preventing the app from initializing.

How to Identify and Fix It If you see the infinite loading screen, follow these steps to confirm:

  1. Open your browser Developer Tools (F12).

  2. Go to the Console tab.

  3. Look for the error: Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 431 (Request Header Fields Too Large).

The Resolution:

To resolve this, you need to clear the session overhead:

  • Clear Cookies & Cache: Specifically for your SFM domain (e.g., *.footprint.sustainability.cloud.sap).

  • Use Incognito/Private Mode: This ensures a clean header state for the session.

We have published a dedicated Knowledge Base Article with the full technical breakdown and official steps. You can find more details here: Link.

By following these simple steps, you can ensure your master data replication flows remain active for your carbon footprint calculations and auditor reviews.

Has anyone else encountered this? Let’s discuss in the comments!

Tags: #SAPSustainability #SFM #ManageReplications #SustainabilityFootprintManagement

More information and example would be highly appreciated.

Thanks,
Filip

entry.title: Re: Requirement – Credit Management for Cement Sales Orders
entry.content:
1. Product Hierarchy / Material Group → Risk Category Mapping
Assign Cement materials to a distinct product hierarchy or material group. Use a custom enhancement (BAdI for credit check) to derive a different risk category when Cement is present in the order. Risk category then drives stricter credit rules (90 days). Other materials retain the standard risk category (180 days). This requires extensibility, since standard FSCM does not evaluate material groups.

2. BAdI Enhancement (most precise)
In S/4HANA Cloud, check released BAdIs such as BADI_SD_CREDIT_CHECK or BADI_SD_CM (names vary by release). There add logic such that if sales order contains Cement material → apply stricter overdue rule (90 days). Else → apply standard rule (180 days). This allows you to keep a single order type and single customer, but still differentiate by material.

entry.title: Update SAP Market Communication
entry.content:
Market Process Management (MPM) with Application Process Engine (APE): Moving Into the Core of SAP S/4HANA Utilities 2025

For several years now, Market Process Management (MPM) together with the Application Process Engine (APE) has been successfully supporting customers in the German and other deregulated energy markets. Introduced to the market in 2023, the solution has proven its robustness, scalability, and ability to handle complex market communication processes with high automation and transparency.

Due to this strong adoption and positive market feedback, SAP has decided to technically integrate MPM with APE directly into SAP S/4HANA Utilities 2025. This means:

  • MPM with APE will become part of the SAP S/4HANA Utilities core
  • No additional add‑on installation will be required moving forward

This strategic step ensures a streamlined architecture, simplifies lifecycle management, and strengthens MPM as the central framework for market communication processes in utilities.

Successor of IDEXGC

With its modern, enhanced capabilities, MPM with APE is the designated successor to IDEXGC.
As part of the shift to SAP S/4HANA Utilities 2025:

  • IDXGC 802 will not be released for SAP S/4HANA 2025–based systems
  • IDXGC will not be installable on S/4HANA 2025
    → This is an important consideration for all customers planning their transformation to SAP S/4HANA.

MPM with APE provides a future‑ready, scalable alternative, fully aligned with the long‑term roadmap of SAP Utilities.

Support for Customers Transitioning from IDXGC

SAP Services actively supports existing IDXGC customers on their transition journey to MPM (APE).
This includes:

  • Functional and technical guidance
  • Migration support
  • Best practices based on real‑world customer experiences

This ensures that utilities can make the shift smoothly while safeguarding continuity in market communication processes.

Additional Information

For further details and clarifications, please refer to the SAP Note:

🔗 SAP Note 1745581 — Additional Information on MPM & APE
https://me.sap.com/notes/1745581/E

entry.title: Re: Contribution Margin Forecast with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Thanks for your interest, Michael - much appreciated.

Let me please answer as follows:

  1. Seamless Planning in BDC enhances the flexibility to decide where to perform calculations according to the use case. E.g., need for persistency such as budget break-downs would still happen via data actions while pure reporting measures (e.g., % of calculations) could be modeled in the Analytic Model. Finally, AI capabilities could be handled in Datasphere (HANA PAL) or via partner solutions (e.g., Databricks via BDC Connect).
  2. Real-time planning is indeed possible but the question is how close to "real-time" you need to get. Theoretically, you can combine purely federated (and in that sense real-time) data with SAC planning data. However, performance aspects in the frontend will determine if you need to have an intermediate snapshot/persistency, which can be close to real-time (steered via replication frequency).
  3. As pointed out under point 1, for specific use cases Data Actions will absolutely remain the best or even only possible solution, thus delays-to-insight driven by SAC Data Action batch processing can still occur. I would focus on performance-optimized data action design so that these could be triggered by end users ad-hoc rather than waiting for batch processes over night or similar.

I hope that clarifies your questions.

Cheers

entry.title: Re: Requirement – Credit Management for Cement Sales Orders
entry.content: Thank you for your valuable suggestion. I understand that for material-specific credit control (e.g., Cement vs other products), standard configuration in SAP S/4HANA FSCM may not be sufficient and enhancement may be required. However, I would request your further guidance on the implementation approach, as I am facing some confusion: 1. You mentioned using Product Hierarchy / Material Group to derive a different Risk Category. o Could you please clarify how and where this mapping is done? o Is this achievable via standard configuration, or does it require a BAdI implementation? 2. Regarding the BAdI approach: o Which exact BAdI should be used (e.g., UKM_CHECK_STEP or any other)? o At what point in the process (Sales Order / Delivery) should the logic be implemented? o How can we dynamically apply different overdue thresholds (90 days for Cement, 180 days for others) within the same customer? 3. Since we are using a single customer and single order type for multiple materials, how can the system differentiate the credit check behavior at material level? 4. Also, is it recommended to: o Use only BAdI-based logic, or o Combine it with a separate order type for Cement for better control? If possible, I would really appreciate if you could provide a step-by-step approach or high-level design, including: • Configuration steps (if any) • Enhancement points • Best practice followed in similar scenarios This will help us implement the solution correctly. Thank you again for your support.

Bug was fixed on 23. March 2026 by SAP. Quickbuilder works with Structures now.

@jie_deng

entry.title: SAP MDG News: AI Use Cases delivered, demoed and presented
entry.content:
Last week, I had the opportunity to present the latest SAP MDG and AI developments to a major client. Since the 2023 release, SAP MDG has unlocked numerous AI use cases, with most integrated directly into Joule and additional embedded AI features available. The live demo showcased all these capabilities in action.

Key AI Use Cases Overview

We're currently seeing three interaction types of Joule with MDG that are transforming how users work with master data:

Use Case 1: Informational

Knowledge at Your Fingertips: With Joule integrated into the SAP Launchpad, business users can ask MDG-related questions without leaving their current workspace. Instead of opening external browsers or applications, users can simply ask questions like "Does MDG support parallel processes for the same object?" Joule accesses the complete SAP Help documentation and SAP Community to deliver high-quality answers instantly.

Use Case 2: Navigational

Smart Data Discovery: Users can pose specific questions about master data within their MDG system. For example, asking "Show me all Business Partners with NWT in their name" displays results directly within the Joule chatbot. Users can then navigate directly from Joule to the appropriate app or action, eliminating the traditional process of opening the correct app, searching for business partners, and then proceeding with tasks.

 

Use Case 3: Transactional

Guided Process Creation: This powerful capability is already available in MDG cloud ready mode. When creating a new Business Partner via Joule, users interact within the chatbot interface, receiving guided assistance with the right questions for BP creation. The system also provides duplicate detection alerts directly within the chat experience

Custom AI Solutions Discussion

Beyond standard use cases, we explored customer-specific implementations using SAP Business AI to enhance master data quality. The concept of an "Attribute Recommendation Agent" could automatically improve data quality through regular evaluation and remediation processes, keeping master data current and accurate through automated quality checks.

Client Feedback Section

The feedback on delivered use cases was very positive! We also discussed exciting possibilities for implementing custom AI use cases with SAP Business AI to further enhance their master data governance processes.

Call to Action

For more detailed insights, check out this comprehensive blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/meet-joule-your-ai-copilot-for-sap-master-data-governance/ba-p/14273811

Meet Joule - Your AI Copilot for SAP Master Data Governance 🤖

Best regards,

Steffen

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: SAP Champions and Mentor tips, new ABAP Keyword and upcoming SAP Inside Tracks!
entry.content:
Featured Blog Posts 

Fiori Elements Navigation Patterns 

SAP Champion Mio Yasutake published a valuable blog post last week, bringing light to Fiori Elements Navigation Patterns Showcase. That are multiple ways to navigate Fiori Elements from one application to another, While the SAPUI5 documentation explains each annotation in detail, it is often focused on code snippets. Which can be difficult sometimes to visualize how each navigation pattern behaves in the UI. To address this, Mio created a repository that demonstrates many Fiori Elements navigation patterns along with their actual UI behavior. On her blog post, you will find the link for the sample inside Github and her detailed explanation on how navigation is initiated and how data is passed to the target app. The repository allows developers to explore real examples, compare patterns like semantic links and intent-based navigation, and follow a simple decision guide to choose the right approach for their scenario. 

Read Mio Yasutake Blog post here 

Essential health checks for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule 

SAP UX specialist Leslie Castle shares in his latest blog post a set of essential health checks for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule, helping ensure your setup is fully functional before onboarding business users. The guide walks through key areas such as architecture validation, principal propagation, content exposure, identity provisioning, and troubleshooting tools, offering step-by-step checks to confirm everything is correctly configured. Performing these checks will allow you to become more confident and leverage the significant benefits of Joule. With real examples and clear testing scenarios, the post helps teams quickly identify and resolve issues, so they can confidently roll out Joule and start leveraging its full business value from day one. 

Read Leslie Castle Blog post here 

OData MCP Proxy 

SAP Mentor Wouter Lemaire, introduces the OData MCP Proxy: an open-source, configuration-driven server that exposes any OData V2 or REST API as MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools, making them instantly accessible to AI assistants like Claude, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible client. In his blog post, Wouter how this proxy runs seamlessly on SAP BTP, leveraging services like Destination Service and XSUAA for secure, scalable integration. He shows in detail the architecture, the whole configuration file structure, transport modes, and how AI agents can intelligently interact with SAP services. In the end of his post, you will find a list five MCP servers he built using this exact pattern, for a different SAP service each. We highly recommend looking into all his blog posts in this series, plus the links he shared for GitHub! 

Read Wouter Lemaire Blog post here 

Brand-new ABAP Keyword 

The last blog post highlight was written by SAP expert Kilian Kilger announcing a brand-new top level ABAP keyword in ABAP CE 2602. Designed to fully leverage the power of SAP HANA, the new “MERGE” command combines multiple database operations, such as INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE-into a single, efficient statement. MERGE is a standard SQL database command. It exists on the HANA database and most other commercial databases. The post explains not only how “MERGE” works conceptually and through practical examples, but also the challenges of introducing new keywords in ABAP, including compatibility with existing macros. Due to its ability to join an internal or database table (or a global temporary table for that matter), it can be a very powerful tool in the hands of an ABAP developer.  You may find additional information in the SAP help article here.  

Read Kilian Kilger Blog post here 

Community News 

Ask me Anything with SAP Champions: Session 2 

In the past editions of the Community Voice, we have mentioned the amazing initiative from the SAP Champions program, the Ask me Anything series, and we are happy to let everyone know that the submission of questions for Session 2 is open! SAP Champion Marietta Lopes is leading the March edition, with more than 20 years of experience in SAP transformation programs, SAP S/4HANA implementations, enterprise architecture, governance frameworks, and digital innovation within the SAP ecosystem. This can be a great opportunity to get amazing advice from her on e.g. Clean Core in SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public), RISE or GROW with SAP, Clean Core Governance & Methodologies and much more. To participate, you can either drop your question on the comments section of her blog post linked below or ask anonymously in the link here. 

Ask me Anything with SAP Champion Marietta 

Hello SAP Community: Blog Series in Beginners Corner 

March also marks the beginning of our new blog post series, the Hello SAP Community! Launched by the SAP Community Advocate Moshe Naveh, the initiative is a way of bringing light to the next generation of SAP professionals, creating a space where they can share more about them, what they have been studying, topics of interest, their vision of the future, how they grow in the era of AI and much more. The initiative has already started inside the Beginners Corner Group at SAP Community, with students from the Bangalore area. Every week the participants will post introduction blog posts about themselves that will allow us to get to know them, learn from them and connect. They will answer preset questions, and every participant will also add their own unique touch, insights and experiences.  Are you interested in participating? Make sure to check Moshe post, to find all the useful links, the preset questions and his contact for more information. 

Hello Community Blog Series  

Hobbies Series: Teaching in a School 

This week's Hobbies Series highlight is by the SAP Community member and engineering student, Abhijna blog post. She shares the story of how she started teaching and learning alongside young students, inspired by her grandfather’s impact as an educator. Two years ago she returned to the school where he was a teacher, with a desire to give back. What started with small interactions, like teaching dance and introducing basic concepts of engineering, quickly turned into meaningful moments of curiosity and connection. Through examples and simple explanations, she discovered that even complex topics can spark understanding when approached the right way. Her experience is a reminder that sometimes, creating impact isn’t about doing something big, but about showing up, sharing knowledge, and becoming a small reason for someone to dream bigger. We highly recommend checking her full story, and maybe you also feel inspired to spread your knowledge with more people around you 

Hobbies Series Abhijna Blog post here 

Events 

March Meet-up Recaps 

March was an amazing month filled with community-driven events all over the world. We would like to highlight some of the great recaps of location group members have shared:  

Starting with SAP Inside Track Joinville in Brazil, which took place on March 14 and was completely full of attendees! With a great recap shared by event organizer Gislaine, the event featured more than 7 sponsors and proved to be a big success. In case you also participated in the event, make sure to share your pictures and feedback in the comment section of Gislaine post or publish your own blog post in the Joinville group. 

Read SIT Joinville recap here 

Jumping to Casablanca, local group organizer Younes Elmir shared highlights from The Joule Effect event held last week at the SAP Casablanca office. Focused on AI topics, Younes described that what stood out most was the exchange between experienced professionals and emerging SAP talent. If you also would like to participate on the next events, you can join the Casablanca group and keep up with the updates. 

Read Casablanca event recap here 

Pune Stammtisch also took place last week, bringing local SAP community members together to discuss topics such as AI in the SAP space, Future of Intelligent ERP and much more. Event organizer Parineeta Somvanshi shared more details about the sessions in his latest recap, but for him the most special aspect was the great conversations that continued between sessions, people exchanging ideas and staying engaged throughout the event. If you are interested on the next upcoming events, or would like to share your own recap impressions, make sure to join the Pune group.  

Read Pune event recap here 

SAP Inside Track Sydney: Save the date 

Talking about opportunities to meet-up, we are happy with the announcement that the SAP Inside Track Sydney dates is finally out! On Friday, September 4, 2026, the event will happen at a new venue, for an elevated experience but with the same community spirit. SAP Champion Mustafa Bensam published the Save the Date on the events calendar page and soon he will publish more details including registration, call for speakers, and sponsorship opportunities. Stay tuned and don´t miss out in the opportunity to meet other Sydney group members, share knowledge and connect. 

SIT Sydney event post here 

SAP Inside Track Recife: Call out for Speakers 

Another great upcoming SIT is the SAP Inside Track Recife, that will happen on May 23 at the Hotel Manibu, in Boa Viagem. Since its first edition in 2026, this year the local group celebrates 10 years of SIT Recife and establishing itself as more than just a tech event. It’s a movement centered around people, experiences, and the continuous growth of the SAP ecosystem in Brazil. The registration for tickets and the agenda will soon to be announced.  But we would like to remind everyone that the Call for Speakers is open until April 10, and you just need to submit your proposal including topic, title and a short abstract to the email: recife@sitbrazil.com. Beyond the event announcement, SAP Champion Marssel Vilaca also have published blog posts with more details on how to apply for speaking and how to become a sponsor.  Check out each one of the posts inside Recife group to find out more details on how to support. 

SIT Recife event post here

entry.title: Re: Resource Handover for Product WTs in S/4HANA Cloud Pu...
entry.content:
Dear All,

For the sake of registering the exact solution:

LOSC is indeed solving the issue, although it requires some kind of HU usage based on my research and testing.

What can be done is tho (to avoid having an HU right at the source): create a product WT, and when picking from the source bin (only works on RF!), you create a pick HU -> this way, the system will modify the dest. bin to the intermediate one (originally WT is created for the final one) and system creates another WT upon confirmation.

--> This way, the handling via 2 seperate resources is possible.

Thank you for all the replies and help!

Best Regards,

Adam

entry.content:
Hi there, I am developing a Fiori app on Cloud Foundry that fetches Currency Exchange Rates from S/4HANA to perform CRUD operations. We are using the Job Scheduler service to trigger specific endpoints daily.

We encountered an issue with the 'Finance - Currency Exchange Rates Integration (SAP_COM_0B18)' communication scenario: it doesn't support Basic Authentication. While we successfully implemented a SAML assertion, it isn't compatible with the Job Scheduler since SAML requires an active user session to generate tokens.

We are now attempting to switch to SSL certificate-based authentication (X.509). However, we have no prior experience with this setup and are struggling to fetch data. Could someone provide guidance on configuring a Communication Arrangement using an SSL certificate and how to properly call the API from our application?

entry.title: loading sequence in EWM public cloud environment
entry.content:
Hi!

I have a question relating the WT creation/confirmation and the loading sequence. So the example is that we have a Freight Order which has lets say 9 deliveries. 3 of them goes to Customer A, other 3 goes to Customer B and the rest 3 goes to Customer C. The truck first will stop at A, then at B then at C. It means that first we need to load the HU-s of C, then in the middle the HU-s of B then the HU-s of A. 

For the business is very important to put the HU-s into the GI-zone in the appropriate sequence so in the line the HU-s of the C have to be in the beginning, because they need to load those HU-s first. (then B and then A) 

My question is that how can I ensure this in Public Cloud environment? From TM we do not get any loading sequence or Truck stop information, we just have the deliveries belonging to one Freight order. The business does not have the time to create and confirm the tasks one by one for a delivery, they need to do in mass (create all tasks for all deliveries under one Freight order). When they select all deliveries and create the tasks, it won't guarantee the appropriate sequence. First they need to confirm the tasks of deliveries which goes to C, then B and then A. 

I have nothing in my mind how should I solve in standard way or without any development.

entry.title: Re: Intercompany return process steps and scope item
entry.content:
Dear @winpavan90,

If the business requirement starts from the plant currently holding the goods and sending them back onward, SAP published Public Cloud guidance does not describe this as a fully mirrored issuing-plant-start version of scope item 2F4. Instead, SAP documents a workaround/general process in which the material is first received into the local plant then moved to the target plant and only afterward a manual return purchase order of type NB2 is created to send the products back to the supplier. SAP also states in SAP Note that using follow-up activity 0004 (Ship to Other Plant) directly when creating the return order is currently not supported in Public Cloud for this scenario

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3569034

Best Regards,

Dawid

Nevertheless, I received a score of only 45%, and I am wondering how this result was calculated. I completed many of the tasks, and based on the score, it seems as if I had left more than half of them undone, which is not the case. I also followed the naming conventions wherever required. I have been working in the SAP environment for 19 years and also provide training. Yes, an exam is different, but at the moment I have many questions. It is possible that I had a blackout, but I did check my settings again where time allowed. I open a ticket to review, because this ist totally strength. Thats my experience. On this way do exercises on the system!!!!!!!

entry.title: What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – March 2026
entry.content:
We’re kicking off 2026 with a new set of innovations in SAP HANA Cloud, continuing our evolution as the database that AI was looking for.

The Q1 2026 release introduces capabilities that enhance automation, flexibility, and intelligent data interaction, from infrastructure expansion and improved scalability to new agent-driven approaches for exploring enterprise data.  Together, these innovations help organizations reduce complexity, optimize performance, and unlock faster insights.

Let’s dive into some of the key highlights of this release.


SAP HANA Cloud agents

Following our announcement at TechEd 2025, we are excited to bring you the Data & Discovery Agent Procedures in SAP HANA Cloud.

As part of our journey toward a more agentic and AI-native data platform, these agent procedures combine knowledge graph–based discovery with AI-driven query generation. This allows users to engage with their data in a more intuitive, conversational way, reducing the need for deep SQL expertise.

With the SAP HANA Cloud Data & Discovery Agents, business users and developers alike can more easily bridge the gap between business questions and complex data models, accelerating access to insights and enabling more efficient data exploration.

To support early adoption and gather valuable feedback, this capability is now available through a Beta Program, with registration starting April 1. We invite you to participate, explore the features firsthand, and help shape the future of agent-driven data interaction in SAP HANA Cloud.

Register here for the Beta Program:
https://url.sap/399tsd 

 

For more information, check out this blogpost by @shabana 

 

Machine Learning and NLP services Updates 

With the Q1 2026 release of SAP HANA Cloud, we continue to strengthen embedded Machine Learning and AI capabilities across the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), and the Natural Language Processing (NLP) services. These enhancements focus on making tabular AI predictions using custom AI models or semantic data retrieval scenario easier to use and applied to real business application data. 

Key highlights of this release are: 

  • A new Text column TFIDF with truncatedSVD transformer operator in AutoML, for simplest and efficient text feature processing for use cases like Service Ticket Intelligence 
  • A new dimension reduction function for stored text embedding vectors, allowing to reduce high cardinality vectors to lower cardinality of 62, 128 or 256 dimensions for faster processing while retaining key semantic insights 
  • A new feature space reduction function Feature Agglomeration, allowing to group correlated feature columns into compact, interpretable clusters, thus reducing feature dimensionality 
  • Support for clustering of Vector columns using the Affinity Propagation clustering function 
  • Faster text classification function, supporting float precision for better performance 
  • Introducing a SQL CROSS_ENCODE function, for more precise semantic re-ranking of search retrieval results using the SAP HANA Cloud NLP services  
  • The python Machine Learning client for SAP HANA 2.28 supporting several new functions like text log parsing, cross encoder and parameter optimization with the multi-task multilayer perception neural network function. 

 

 

 

Calculation View Modeling 

As with every release, numerous new capabilities and enhancements were added to Calculation View Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud. One of the highlights clearly is the ability to semantically import SAP HANA Cloud Calculation Views into SAP Business Data Cloud, for reuse purpose without data duplication.

To learn more about additional Calculation View Modeling innovations, check out this blogpost by JanZwickel.

Reuse Calculation Views in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) 

Combined, SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud are a strong foundation for your data and analytics use cases.
Semantic onboarding in SAP Business Data Cloud enables calculation views from SAP HANA Cloud to be leveraged in BDC without transferring data, only semantics are transferred, creating BDC proxies.
As such, we enable uses to re-use existing investments made into calculation views, in SAP BDC. Moreover, we foster a HANA-native approach of modeling, tightly integrated with BDC capabilities; enabling you to extend our HANA-based data marts with SAP BDC. Untimely, allowing you to benefit from both solutions in combination.

AT the moment, two integration modes are available: 

  • Flat View: Onboards views as flat structures (not for Dimension Views). Use for minimal BDC enhancements. 
  • Construction Kit: Imports only private attributes, allowing flexible recombination in BDC. Supports Dimension Views. 

Onboarded semantics include input parameters, variables, currency/unit conversion, labels, hierarchies, and information about star-join dimensions. Further enhancements are planned, and roadmap is driven by customer feedback.

Are you eager to adopt the bi-directional integration between SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud? If yes, learn more about our SAP HANA Cloud Customer Care Program down below.

 

SAP HANA Cloud Customer Care Program

Calculation Views in the Context of SAP Business Data Cloud

To further support customers and partners exploring integration scenarios between SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud, we are introducing a dedicated SAP HANA Cloud Customer Care Program focused Calculation Views in the Context of SAP Business Data Cloud

This program is designed to help participants explore how existing SAP HANA Cloud calculation views can be leveraged in the context of SAP Business Data Cloud, particularly through semantic onboarding, which enables reuse of models without requiring data replication. In addition, participants can evaluate scenarios such as building new calculation views on top of SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products.

Through this initiative, we aim to work closely with customers and partners who are actively working on, or planning, projects in this space. Participants will have the opportunity to gain early insights into new capabilities, receive guidance during implementation, and provide direct feedback that helps shape future enhancements.

Participation in the program is limited and based on alignment with the program scope and project requirements.

If you are interested in joining the Customer Care Program, stay tuned to this blogpost and our #whatsnewinsaphanacloud hashtag as we will open the registration soon!

 

SQL Tools for Administration in SAP HANA Cloud Central 

SQL Console Code Completion 

With the Q1 Release, the SQL console in HANA Cloud Central now includes enhanced code completion that suggests both SQL snippets and schema objects as you type, making it quicker and easier to work with SQL. As users begin typing, the console will display recommended snippets such as CREATE TABLE, CREATE PROCUDURE, or GRANT. When the user selects a statement, it will insert a complete template automatically. It also provides lookups for tables, views, and columns within the current schema, reducing errors, and helping users find the objects they need. These improvements boost productivity for experienced users and make the SQL experience far more approachable for those who are new to SQL. 

 

SQL Console GenAI 

The SQL Console now includes new GenAI‑powered actions that make working with SQL faster and more accessible. Within the Run menu, users will find three new options: Run Prompt, Explain SQL Statement using AI, and Optimize SQL Statement using AI.  

By selecting text in the SQL Console and choosing Run Prompt, users can generate SQL statements directly from natural‑language instructions. The Explain option provides clear natural‑language explanations of SQL statements, including complex queries, while Optimize SQL Statement uses AI to refine and improve existing SQL for better performance. These enhancements help users work more efficiently and make SQL much easier for those who are new to it. 

 

SAP HANA Cloud Clients 

Addition of the SAP HANA database client to the package manager for .NET 

The SAP HANA database client is officially available on NuGet, making it easier than ever for developers to integrate SAP HANA Cloud into their applications. By adding the SAP-provided packages directly from the SAP profile and NuGet, developers can quickly include both the HANA .NET driver and Entity Framework Core provider without needing manual downloads or custom setup. Users can also now see the updated tutorials on Connecting Using the Microsoft Entity Framework Core (EF Core) and SAP HANA .NET Interface. 

Direct connection option for replicas 

The SAP HANA Client supports a direct connection option for replicas, enabling applications to explicitly route read-only workloads away from the primary system. Developers can now use a SQL hint such as WITH HINT (RESULT_LAG('hana_sr')) to target replica nodes for read‑only queries or specify the connection property replicationRole=REPLICA to ensure the connection is established directly with a replica. This capability improves cost efficiency and performance by offloading analytical workloads from the primary system while still maintaining full high‑availability readiness. We also have this new tutorial on Routing queries to a read only replica. 

 

New installation program for the data lake client  

The data lake client now includes a new installation program based on the SAP HANA database lifecycle manager, providing a more unified and streamlined setup experience. This installer will be available through the SAP Software Center and supports Microsoft Windows, Linux x64, and Linux ARM64. When downloaded from the SAP Software Center, it also includes the required cryptographic libraries, simplifying deployment, and ensuring secure operation. This harmonized installation process makes it easier for customers to install and manage clients for SAP HANA Cloud. 

 

Enabling HTTP proxy support for the data lake client 

The data lake client now supports connecting through an HTTP proxy, enabling better compatibility with enterprise network environments. Users can enable proxy usage by configuring the settings proxyHTTP=true, along with proxyHost, proxyPort, proxyUser, and proxyPassword. These options allow the client to route traffic through an organization’s proxy infrastructure while maintaining secure and controlled access. This enhancement ensures the data lake client fits seamlessly into enterprise deployments that rely on HTTP proxy requirements. 

 

Performance Analyzer in SAP HANA Cloud Central

With the Q1 2026 release, SAP HANA Cloud Central introduces the Performance Analyzer, a new capability for analyzing historical performance data of SAP HANA Cloud database instances.

The Performance Analyzer provides access to up to 42 days of historical metrics, enabling administrators to identify performance issues, investigate root causes, and correlate system behavior over time. This complements real-time monitoring by adding the historical visibility required for effective troubleshooting.

Key capabilities include:

  • Load History Chart
    A central entry point for analysis, allowing users to identify anomalies, select time ranges, and correlate performance changes with system events across a 42-day history.
  • Chart Library
    A collection of predefined visualizations across categories such as CPU, memory, locks, transactions, and workload, which can be added dynamically to analysis views.
  • Preset and Custom Views
    Predefined views for common scenarios such as CPU, memory, and lock analysis, with the ability to create and save custom views tailored to specific needs.
  • Interactive exploration
    Support for tooltips, synchronized time selection, and zooming, enabling detailed analysis of specific time periods.

The Performance Analyzer is integrated across SAP HANA Cloud Central and can be accessed through multiple entry points, including the side navigation, instance overview, alerts, command palette, and Joule.

Typical use cases include analyzing CPU utilization, investigating memory consumption, and troubleshooting performance degradation by comparing historical system behavior.

To learn more, please check out this blogpost by @Hai 

 

Data Integration 

Smart Data Access: Support for a New Cloud MySQL Remote Source Through the Data Access Agent 

With the Smart Data Access (SDA) Data Access Agent introduced in QRC 03/2025, readonly access to a new remote source, MySQL in the cloud, will be available starting in QRC 01/2026. 

Configuration and usage remain the same as for previously supported remote sources. For newly provisioned Data Access Agents, all new adapters are activated by default. To activate new adapters on an already provisioned Data Access Agent, refer to SAP Note 2600176, SAP HANA Smart Data Access Supported Remote Sources, for details on activation and support information. 

 

Elasticity 

Elastic Compute Node: Automated ECN Provisioning and Deprovisioning Through ECN Policy 

SAP HANA Cloud introduces a significant enhancement to Elastic Compute Node management with the new ECN policy feature, available starting with QRC 01/2026. Previously, ECN Advisor recommendations required manual action. With ECN policies, the system can now automatically provision and deprovision ECNs based on your workload patterns. After running the ECN Advisor, you can choose scheduled scaling for predictable usage or demand based scaling for more dynamic environments. 

Scheduled scaling allows you to define recurring timebased rules to add or remove ECNs. Demand based scaling adjusts capacity automatically according to system load, and the initial release provides two scaling focuses, Availability and Cost. These options determine how aggressively the system reacts to load changes, with the ability to scale up to sixteen ECNs sequentially. Table replication continues to require separate handling, and each instance supports one demand based policy with the option to configure multiple scheduled policies. More details can be found in the About ECN Policies section. 

 

Further news:

SAP HANA Cloud Datacenter expansion 

In Q1 2026, we are expanding the global availability of SAP HANA Cloud in four new BTP regions: 

  • Germany (Frankfurt) and UK (London) on Microsoft Azure, 
  • Japan (Tokyo) and US East (Sterling) on SAP Cloud Infrastructure (SCI). 

With these additions, SAP HANA Cloud is now available in more than 40 public cloud locations worldwide. While most regions run on hyperscalers, SAP HANA Cloud continues to strengthen its footprint on SAP Cloud Infrastructure (SCI), which now spans seven regions (see Figure 1). This expansion provides customers with greater flexibility to choose both the infrastructure provider and deployment region that best align with their business, performance, and regulatory requirements. 

EU22—the second Microsoft Azure region in the EU—lays the foundation for future multi-region disaster recovery within the EU. Similarly, US01—the second SCI presence in the United States—prepares the groundwork for future multi-region disaster recovery capabilities in the US. For the latest updates on the availability timeline for multi-region disaster recovery in these regions, please refer to SAP Roadmap Explorer. 

SAP HANA Cloud Public Cloud Locations  

 

Expanded Performance Class Options Across Hyperscalers

SAP HANA Cloud continues to enhance flexibility in sizing and performance across supported infrastructures.

On Alibaba Cloud, SAP HANA database instances now support additional performance classes and instance sizes. With the QRC 01/2026 update, you can provision configurations tailored to different workload requirements, ranging from memory-intensive scenarios with up to 8 TB of memory to more compute-focused setups. This provides greater flexibility to align system sizing with specific application needs.
More details about the available options can be found SAP HANA Database Size.  

On AWS, a new 16 GB instance size is now available, designed specifically for low-throughput and lightweight workloads. This option enables more efficient deployment of smaller applications, development and testing environments, and cost-sensitive use cases, helping avoid overprovisioning.

This instance size will also be made available on Google Cloud Platform and Microsoft Azure, further extending consistent sizing flexibility across all hyperscalers supported by SAP HANA Cloud.

 

 

Thanks for taking the time to explore the What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud on Q1 2026 innovations!

If you’d like to go deeper into the technical details of the March 2026 release, we recommend visiting the What’s New Viewer in the technical documentation. It provides a comprehensive overview of the full release scope. To stay informed about upcoming innovations, announcements, and best practices, follow the SAP HANA Cloud tag.

You can also find our latest blog posts by searching for the #whatsnewinsaphanacloud hashtag.

If you missed earlier What’s New webinars, you’ll find recordings of past sessions and upcoming events in our YouTube playlist. Our What’s New webinar covering the Q1 2026 innovations will be posted on this playlist very soon.

Do you have questions about SAP HANA Cloud or want to share your thoughts on the new features?
Join the conversation in the SAP HANA Cloud Community Q&A or leave a comment below. We’d love to hear from you!

entry.title: Implementing Custom Logic to Include Project Information in Billing Email Template
entry.content:
Team,

We are currently working on a business scenario similar to the one described in the following SAP documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EXTENSIBILITY_EXPLORER/397bf26514884cc8a5ece65970e8e3e6/3f898d6f59254ef497f00c78423cec7a.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

Our business requirement is to include Event ID / Event Description (Project ID / Project Description) in the invoice email template that is sent to our customers.

We have followed the implementation steps described in the SAP documentation. However, we are currently facing difficulties at Step 4 of the implementation.

According to the documentation, we need to create a new custom logic (for example, Project Info in Billing Email Template) in the Sales: Sales Document business context using BAdI Sales Header Modification.

However, the current version of the Custom Fields and Logic app requires us to select both:

  • Business Context

  • Business Object Node

    In order to determine the extension points where the logic should be implemented.

    We were able to select Business Context: "Sales: Sales Document", but we are unsure which extension point should be selected to implement the logic for the custom field "Customer Project ID".

    When selecting this business context, the system displays 23 different extension points, and it is unclear which one should be used for this scenario.

    Could you please advise which specific extension point should be selected so that we can implement the custom logic to populate the Customer Project ID field for use in the billing email template?

    For your reference, we have attached a screenshot showing the available extension points after selecting the Sales: Sales Document business context.

    Please note that this field is very important for our business, as the Project ID / Project Description must be visible in the invoice email template sent to customers. Therefore, any guidance on the correct extension point and implementation approach would be greatly appreciated.

    We kindly request your support in resolving this issue at the earliest convenience.

entry.title: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll – Continuously Updated Resource
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

SAP has published an important and continuously updated blog on the long‑term modernization of SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP). It provides a clear overview of SAP’s plans to move ECP to a modern technology foundation and introduce new capabilities.

🔗 Blog link:
Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/modernizing-sap-successfactors-employee-central-payroll/ba-p/14002355 

Key highlights from the blog

✨ Modern technical foundation
SAP is transitioning ECP to a more advanced platform based on S/4HANA and ABAP Platform 2023, enabling higher performance, scalability, and long‑term innovation.

🤖 New and upcoming features
SAP plans to introduce:

  • AI‑enabled capabilities such as “Explain Pay,”
  • Enhanced Payroll Control Center capabilities,
  • The updated HORIZON UI for a unified user experience.

⚙️ Operational improvements
Customers can expect enhancements such as high‑availability production setup and planned improved encryption.

Why this blog is worth following

📌 The content is updated regularly, and SAP continues to add new details as the modernization progresses.
📌 The comments section is very active—many community members and SAP experts share additional explanations, examples, and useful links. It’s a great place to learn from real discussions and clarifications.

I found this an excellent resource for staying up‑to‑date with SAP’s roadmap for ECP.

💬 Has anyone already reviewed the new modernization plans or started preparing internally?
Would be great to hear your thoughts, experiences, or questions!

Best regards

Tatiana

entry.title: Trusted Master Data to AI-Ready Insights with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
This demo highlights how trusted master data managed in SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) becomes ready-to-use data products in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) and can be consumed across analytics and AI/ML platforms.

Watch a demo of ensuring trusted data products with SAP MDG and SAP BDC

Starting in the BDC cockpit, the demo shows installation and inspection of SAP-delivered data packages and intelligent apps that include master-data products for customer, product, and supplier. Opening a customer data product reveals rich metadata—data and source properties, related packages, and a detailed object view. The demo also walks through lineage and impact visualizations so you can trace where data originated and how it relates to other objects.

A key capability shown is zero-copy data sharing: BDC data products can be shared without duplication to SAP and non‑SAP systems. The demo shows the customer data product already shared with SAP Databricks as well as other targets. In Databricks, the shared product appears in the catalog ready for enrichment with AI and ML. The same data product is automatically available in SAP Datasphere’s Data Builder for modeling and integration into a business data fabric. There it can be combined with other datasets to create custom, customer-managed data products for analytical use cases.

Overall, the demo demonstrates an end-to-end flow: high-quality master data from MDG is packaged and documented in BDC, transparently traced with lineage and impact, and shared seamlessly to Databricks, Datasphere, and external systems for analytics and AI-driven scenarios.

Recommended Resources 

Blog Post: What is a Business Data Fabric? 

Blog Post: What are data products? 

Best Practice Article: The converged data imperative: How to feed AI, govern data, and secure the future 

Blog Post: Master data is a strategic, business-critical asset 

Hands-on: SAP Business Data Cloud basic trial

entry.title: What is a data steward?
entry.content:
A data steward is the business-facing guardian of an organization’s data: the person who turns fragmented, messy information into reliable, understandable, and defensible assets. They operate at the intersection of business and technology, ensuring that data is fit for purpose for reporting, analytics, and AI while making it easy for people across the organization to find, interpret, and trust the numbers they use to make decisions.

What they do

Rather than a long list of tasks, think of a data steward’s work as three connected threads. First, they set and enforce standards: defining business terms, documenting lineage, and embedding governance so answers are reproducible and explainable. Second, they improve quality and access by profiling data, fixing or escalating issues, and curating business-ready data products that reduce manual preparation. Third, they enable people—collaborating with IT, domain experts, analysts, and governance teams to ensure the right controls, ownership, and context are in place so decisions can be defended under scrutiny.

Why it matters

Without stewardship, organizations face competing answers, wasted time reconciling datasets, and increased regulatory or reputational risk. With effective stewardship, teams spend less time validating numbers and more time acting on insights. Trusted data shortens time-to-insight, reduces duplicate work, and makes advanced uses of data—like production AI—both safer and more reliable.

Common challenges

Data stewards contend with legacy debt, siloed systems, and tool sprawl that force manual stitching and reconciliation. Governance is often seen as a blocker rather than a safeguard, and many tools create visual outputs without preserving lineage or enforceable ownership. The most effective stewards focus on building governance into workflows, so documentation, monitoring, and lineage are captured automatically instead of retrofitted.

Tools and approaches that help

Practical stewardship relies on active metadata and an enterprise data catalog to make assets discoverable and transparent, a unified semantic layer so everyone shares meanings and metrics, and master data management to keep core reference data consistent. Governance capabilities that are embedded in the data lifecycle, plus automated profiling, monitoring, and AI-assisted mapping of lineage, reduce manual effort and make controls scalable. The goal is to simplify architecture rather than add yet another layer of tools.

Skills and traits

Successful data stewards combine domain knowledge, attention to detail, and technical fluency with strong communication and stakeholder influence. They translate between business questions and technical requirements, prioritize fixes based on impact, and run data literacy programs so users can interpret and defend results.

 Getting started (practical first moves)

  • Inventory key sources and stakeholders and agree on a small set of core business definitions.
  • Profile data to find the highest-impact quality issues and capture lineage and ownership.
  • Put cataloging, basic monitoring, and governance rules in place, then iterate based on usage and outcomes.

In short, a data steward makes data discoverable, explainable, and defensible—embedding governance so teams can move quickly without sacrificing trust. With the right mix of tools and practices, stewardship transforms data from a liability into an asset that supports faster, safer decision-making and more reliable AI.

Recommended Resources 

  • Blog Post: What is a Business Data Fabric? 
  • Blog Post: What are data products? 
  • Best Practice Article: The converged data imperative: How to feed AI, govern data, and secure the future 
  • Blog Post: Master data is a strategic, business-critical asset 
  • Hands-on: SAP Business Data Cloud basic trial

entry.title: How to create connection for Presto simba driver
entry.content:
How to create connection for Presto Simba driver?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create connection for Presto simba driver
entry.content:
Download and install the Simba ODBC driver for Google BigQuery, and configure the driver based on your Windows or Linux platform.
Find driver downloads for the Magnitude Simba driver for BigQuery and access to documentation on the Google Cloud website.

Select the link Windows 64-bit (msi) or Linux 32-bit and 64-bit (tar.gz) to start the installation.
After you install the driver, follow the instructions to configure the driver for either a data source name (DSN) connection or a server name (DSN-less) connection. Then create the Google BigQuery ODBC datastore.

You can check the documentation below for more information:

  • Download and install the Simba ODBC driver
  • Download and install the Simba ODBC driver - Google BigQuery
